qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Malformed multipart cleanup triggers stack-overflow DoS
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: GHSA-xf7r-hgr6-v32p fixes orphaned temp files after an error races with an async `fileFilter` callback by short-circuiting inside `lib/make-middleware.js`; this finding instead depends on recursive `removeUploadedFiles()` cleanup plus synchronous `MemoryStorage._removeFile()` causing a stack overflow, and the advisory patch does not touch those functions.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described orphaned-file cleanup bug. The only qualifying finding is a separate recursive-cleanup stack-overflow DoS in the same package version.
evidence: The advisory patch commit `739919097dde3921ec31b930e4b9025036fa74ee` is titled `fix orphaned files issue` and adds an `if (errorOccured) { ... fileStream.resume() }` guard inside the `fileFilter` callback path in `lib/make-middleware.js`.
The advisory patch adds `test/async-file-filter-orphan.js`, which verifies that malformed multipart input no longer leaves orphaned temp files after an async `fileFilter` callback.

The finding report and PoC target a different root cause: `removeUploadedFiles()` recursively calls `handleFile(idx + 1)` and, with synchronous `MemoryStorage._removeFile()`, attacker-controlled file counts exhaust the JavaScript stack and trigger `RangeError: Maximum call stack size exceeded`.
